PostSnail
Admin Forest Manifesto Docs Media Kit

Guide

Publish On Cloudflare Pages

PostSnail exports plain static files, so Cloudflare Pages can host the microblog without a server bill.

Save Your ShellExport Website ZIPUploadCheck Proof FilesRegister In Forest

0. Save Your Shell

Before publishing, export an encrypted .postsnail Shell and keep it private. This is your editable source; do not upload it to Cloudflare Pages.

1. Export Website ZIP

In the admin Generate tab, unlock your key and click Export Website ZIP. Use the Verify tab before uploading.

2. Upload

Create a Cloudflare Pages project and upload the unzipped bundle contents. The deployed root should contain index.html, postsnail.manifest.json, and .well-known/postsnail.json.

3. Check Proof Files

Open the deployed .well-known/postsnail.json and postsnail.manifest.json URLs in the browser. They must be public HTTPS URLs for Forest to verify them.

4. Register In Forest

Go to PostSnail Forest, paste your public homepage URL, and wait for the status to move from queued to indexed. Forest indexes summaries only, not full post bodies.

© 2026 Boaz Alhadeff. PostSnail is Apache-2.0 licensed; redistributed copies must preserve NOTICE attribution. See Legal and Third-party notices.
